title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Haversham, Huishe, and Gwendolyn Vane discuss evidence suggesting that Mrs. Aigle may have given money to Jack Aigle, which would support Jack's claim that his mother provided the funds. During a tense moment, Corston Lindström attempts to calm Hester Aigle, but Hester flees the room, followed by a loud bang at the front door. Philip Darntes suggests the truth may never be known, while Tina claims she knows nothing about the situation.

In a subsequent meeting, Inspector Rush (Huishe) reports to the Chief of Police and the Commissioner. He states that while there is no evidence sufficient for a court conviction, the investigation has narrowed the suspects. Rush concludes that Mrs. Aigle was killed between 7:15 and 7:30 PM and posits that the murder was a crime of passion rather than a premeditated act. He identifies Leo Aigle and Gwendolyn Vane as the primary suspects with motives. Rush suggests Leo may have killed her with a poker to stage a robbery, or Gwendolyn may have acted on impulse to frame Jacqueline to secure her relationship with Leo. Rush notes that Leo might resent the affection his wife showed his non-biological son.

Huishe also expresses suspicion regarding Hester Aigle, noting her tension and suggesting she may be worried about a young person named Hirst. Additionally, Huishe mentions a black-haired girl from the county library who appears anxious and may possess information, as well as a man named Mike, whose whereabouts near Mingchen Mountain at the time of the murder cannot be independently verified.
